items.title: Select Small Non-Coding RNAs Are Determinants of Survival in Older Adults.
Taming Momentum: Rethinking Optimizer States Through Low-Rank Approximation
Preference Packing: Efficient Preference Optimization for Large Language Models

passed_items.title: Select Small Non-Coding RNAs Are Determinants of Survival in Older Adults.
passed_items.abstract: To investigate the relevance of small RNAs to human longevity, we pursued three goals: (a) to validate epigenetic (small RNA) factors underlying survival of older adults, (b) to develop and validate prediction models of survival for potential clinical application, and (c) to identify plausible druggable targets prolonging longevity. We evaluated 828 small non-coding RNAs-687 microRNAs (miRNAs) and 141 piwi-interacting RNAs (piRNAs)-in baseline plasma from 1271 community-dwelling older adults (вүҘвҖү71вҖүyears) in the Duke-EPESE study. Our predictive model incorporating smRNAs, clinical variables (demographics, lifestyle, mood, physical function, standard clinical laboratory tests, NMR-derived lipids and metabolites, and medical conditions) and age achieved strong performance, with cross-validated AUCs of 0.92 for 2-year survival in Discovery and 0.87 in external Validation. Nine piRNAs, all reduced in longer-lived individuals, were identified as potential therapeutic targets. Under the assumption of causal sufficiency, these data provide causal evidence linking circulating small RNAs with survival outcomes in humans. While such inference does not replace experimental validation, it complements mechanistic studies by identifying candidate molecular drivers most relevant to human longevity. Supporting biological plausibility, reduced piRNA biogenesis has been shown to double lifespan in C elegans. Together, our findings identify circulating piRNAs and miRNAs as promising biomarkers and potential therapeutic targets to advance human longevity.
passed_items.raw_text:
Select Small NonвҖҗCoding

Results

Our analyses are based on 1271 participants from the communityвҖҗbased DukeвҖҗEstablished Populations for Epidemiologic Studies of the Elderly (DвҖҗEPESE) cohort, all aged вүҘвҖү71вҖүyears at the time of blood sample collection in year six of the study (1992вҖ“93). In brief, DвҖҗEPESE was a multiвҖҗyear study sponsored by the National Institute on Aging to describe and identify predictors of mortality and investigate risk factors for chronic diseases and loss of functioning (Blazer and GeorgeВ 2012). The North Carolina cohort, established in 1986, was a sample of persons 65вҖүyears or older residing in households in five counties in the Central Piedmont area. This unique NC cohort, with over 50% Black participants at baseline and drawn from a region encompassing diverse racial and urbanвҖ“rural populations, enabled comparisons of risk factors and disease distribution across these groups.

To discover and validate plasma smRNAs (piRNAs and miRNAs) with predictive and causal relevance for survival, we employed a rigorous, multiвҖҗstage protocol to ensure unbiased model selection, error estimation, and generalizability (FigureВ 1). We used nextвҖҗgeneration sequencing to quantify smRNA levels in 707 participants from the DвҖҗEPESE cohort (Kraus etВ al.В 2022). This вҖңExpanded DiscoveryвҖқ dataset comprised a random half of the DвҖҗEPESE cohort with available plasma biospecimens and, was originally selected using stratified randomization to ensure balance by race, sex, age, and survival status (Methods S1). In the first stage of discovery and validation, the Expanded Discovery dataset was further randomly divided into a вҖңDiscovery SubsetвҖқ dataset (nвҖү=вҖү505) and an вҖңInternal ValidationвҖқ dataset (nвҖү=вҖү202) (FigureВ 1). All data splits preserved the proportion of surviving and nonвҖҗsurviving participants across both the inner and outer loops (see SectionВ 3). Using the Discovery Subset dataset, we built predictive models incorporating 828 smRNAs, age, or 186 clinical features, and their combinations, to predict survival across 2вҖҗ, 5вҖҗ, and 10вҖҗyear intervals following the timepoint of plasma acquisition used for profiling smRNA. The 186 clinical features consisted of sociodemographic variables including demographics and anthropometric measures (age, race, sex, education, income, body mass index [BMI]), medical morbidities, selfвҖҗrated health, depression, health behaviors (smoking habits, alcohol use, sleep), cognitive and physical/motor activity status, and soluble analytes including 48 traditional medical blood tests, 6 additional inflammation parameters, and 48 lipoprotein biomarkers (NMR LipoProfile blood test) (Kraus etВ al.В 2022).

Analytical study design. (A) Leveraging molecular and clinical data from 1271 participants in the DвҖҗEPESE study, we built models to identify predictors and causes of survival at different time horizons (2вҖҗ, 5вҖҗ, and 10вҖҗyears) following best practices for complex statistical machine learning modeling to ensure unbiased and generalizable estimation of predictive performance and causal relevance. The Expanded Discovery dataset (NвҖү=вҖү707; smRNA sequenced in one batch) was split into a Discovery Subset (NвҖү=вҖү505) and an Internal Validation set (NвҖү=вҖү202), while an independent External Validation set (NвҖү=вҖү564), drawn from a separately withheld half of the DвҖҗEPESE cohort and sequenced in a different batch, was used to evaluate model performance, generalizability, and robustness to smRNA measurement variability. (B) Summary of the analytical design. Symbols in blue denote predictive modeling analyses and in green denote causal modeling analyses. Predictive Modeling was conducted to build and validate predictive models for longevity based on smRNA with and without other patient characteristics. For the first stage model discovery and internal validation, we aimed to establish internal validity of our models given smRNA data measured within the same assay batch. We used the Discovery Subset to build predictive models and conduct model selection (inner loop of the stratified repeated nested crossвҖҗvalidation [NCV]). For the second stage of model discovery, we aimed to establish external validity of our models, specifically, the generalizability of our models across different (nonвҖҗoverlapping) batches of biological samples and assays. We used the Expanded Discovery data for model selection and performance estimation via NCV and built the final predictive model (with the best hyperparameters identified), and then subsequently and independently tested the model on independent External Validation data (NвҖү=вҖү564) where smRNAs were sequenced as a separate batch. A label permutation test was conducted to assess potential error estimation biases. Causal Modeling was conducted to identify potential proximal causes (local pathways) for survival using the Expanded Discovery data with Markov Boundary analysis. The causal effects of the identified causes were also estimated. Sepset analyses were conducted to gain more biological insights into the causal role of the study variables. These results were crossвҖҗchecked with prior findings from the literature. Panel A created in BioRender, agreement FD287GWPQ8.

Models were trained on the Discovery Subset dataset (nвҖү=вҖү505), with unbiased performance estimates obtained from the outer loop of nested crossвҖҗvalidation (NCV) and from the Internal Validation dataset (nвҖү=вҖү202). The smRNAвҖҗonly model for 2вҖҗyear survival achieved crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.89вҖүВұвҖү0.02 in the Discovery Subset and validation AUC 0.91 (95% CI: 0.84вҖ“0.98) in the independent Internal Validation dataset (TableВ S1), demonstrating strong predictive performance of smRNAs for 2вҖҗyear survival in the Expanded Discovery data overall. The smRNAвҖҗonly models demonstrated lower accuracy for 5вҖҗ and 10вҖҗyear predictions, yielding AUCs 0.63 and 0.65, respectively, in the independent Internal Validation dataset (TableВ S1). Models incorporating smRNAs, age, and clinical features improved predictive accuracy for 5вҖҗyear survival (AUC 0.77) but showed limited enhancement of 10вҖҗyear predictions (AUC 0.66). These findings highlight the strong shortвҖҗterm predictive value of smRNAs while emphasizing the need for integrated models to improve survival predictions over extended timeframes. Additional predictive performance details for other models and combinations can be found in TableВ S1.

As part of the discovery processвҖ”and to simplify clinical testing for optimal quality and consistencyвҖ”we compared the performance of the initial Qiagen platformвҖҗspecific output (smRNAPSO) with data normalized using the standard miRNA normalization method, smRNATMM (normalized Trimmed Mean of MвҖҗvalues [TMM values]) (Robinson and OshlackВ 2010). Across both the Discovery Subset and Internal Validation datasets, smRNAPSO and smRNATMM demonstrated statistically comparable predictive performance (pвҖү>вҖү0.05) for most outcomes (TableВ S2). Given its simplicity and greater practicality for clinical use, we selected smRNAPSO for all subsequent analyses to streamline future clinical applications.

To strengthen bias prevention and to assess generalizability associated with sequencing batch effects, a doubleвҖҗholdout design was instituted (FigureВ 1). In addition to the nested cross validation followed by internal validation described above, an external validation stage was implemented. To create a separate group of individuals, distinct from the data used to build the model to assess the model's performance, generalizability, and robustness to variability in smRNA measurements, we used an independent sample of 564 participants from DвҖҗEPESE. These individuals were drawn from the remaining randomly withheld half of the DвҖҗEPESE cohort; the plasmas of the independent External Validation cohort were processed and sequenced for smRNA in a separate batch, using the same methods as for the Expanded Discovery dataset but with a slightly greater depth of sequencing (see SectionВ 3). Descriptive statistics in TableВ 1 present clinical features for each DвҖҗEPESE sample dataset; descriptive statistics in TableВ S3 present clinical features and smRNAs of Markov Boundaries (MBs, i.e., from causal analysis) for the Expanded Discovery dataset by survival status (alive/dead) for each time horizon. Methods followed established best practices (Aliferis and SimonВ 2024a, 2024c) (see SectionВ 3 and Methods S1), including an outcome label permutation test (Ojala and GarrigaВ 2010) (FigureВ 1, TableВ S4) to confirm unbiasedness. This comprehensive protocolвҖ”integrating model selection and fitting, error estimation, bias avoidance, and causal interpretationвҖ”validated smRNAs as robust and clinically useful biomarkers of survival. Results are described below for the Expanded Discovery cohort (nвҖү=вҖү707) and the independent External Validation cohort (nвҖү=вҖү564).

Characteristics of DвҖҗEPESE cohort, sample subsets and clinical features selected in Markov Boundary (MB) analyses.

Note: Except where indicated, all measures were obtained at P3 (third inвҖҗperson visit with blood acquisition in 1992); P2 inвҖҗperson visit 1989; SDвҖү=вҖүstandard deviation; IADLвҖҗmotorвҖү=вҖү# motor functionвҖҗrelated Instrumental Activities of Daily Living (IADL) items CAN do (traveling, shopping, preparing meals, doing housework); IADLвҖҗcognitiveвҖү=вҖү# cognitive functionвҖҗrelated Instrumental Activities of Daily Living (IADL) items CAN do (telephone use, taking medications, managing finances).

Abbreviations: HDL, high density lipoprotein; NA, not applicable as the feature was not selected in any model.

1вҖү=вҖүneed no help, 2вҖү=вҖүneed help, 3вҖү=вҖүunable to do.

For 2вҖҗyear survival (TableВ 2 and TableВ S4 with full details), the predictive model built with smRNAPSO data achieved nested crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.01 in the Expanded Discovery dataset (nвҖү=вҖү707) and AUC 0.82 (0.78, 0.87) in the independent External Validation dataset (nвҖү=вҖү564) that underwent smRNA sequencing in a separate batch. The predictive performance of smRNAPSO was significantly better (pвҖү<вҖү0.05) than models built with age or clinical variables alone: age crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.57вҖүВұвҖү0.03 and External Validation AUC 0.56 (0.50, 0.62); and clinical variables crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.79вҖүВұвҖү0.02 and External Validation AUC 0.74 (0.69, 0.80). Compared with models using smRNAPSO alone, combining smRNAPSO, age, and clinical variables resulted in improved predictive performance in the crossвҖҗvalidated Discovery (AUC 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01, pвҖү=вҖү0.03), but comparable performance in the External Validation dataset (AUC 0.87 [0.83, 0.90], pвҖү=вҖү0.35).

Performance of 2вҖҗyear survival models with and without optimization for clinical deployment.

Note: Performances were estimated in the outer loop of the nested cross validation (NCV) of the Expanded Discovery (nвҖү=вҖү707) data (NCV AUC) and independently on the External Validation (NвҖү=вҖү564) data (External Validation AUC).

Abbreviations: GLLвҖҗKвҖҗ3, Generalized local learning with maxвҖҗkвҖү=вҖү3; GLM, generalized linear model, specifically logistic regression.

The six piRNAs are PIRвҖҗ DQ573780, DQ587552, DQ587821, DQ593963, DQ597786, DQ575235.

The seven clinical factors are total high density lipoprotein particle number (Total HDLвҖҗP), instrumental activities of daily living related to physical function (IADLвҖҗmotor), albumin, health index score (continuous measure at P3 time of blood draw), gammaвҖҗglutamyl transferase (GGT), monocytes (absolute count), pack years of cigarette smoking (selfвҖҗreported at T2, the second telephone interview).

The 5 smRNA+2 clinical variables are PIRвҖҗ DQ573780, DQ587821, DQ593963, DQ597786, DQ57523, IADLвҖҗMOTOR, and Total HDLвҖҗP.

For 5вҖҗyear survival (TableВ S4), most models showed lower predictive performance than for 2вҖҗyear survival. Models with smRNAPSO, age, or clinical variables achieved independent External Validation AUCs 0.61 (0.57, 0.66), 0.64 (0.59, 0.68), and 0.74 (0.68, 0.77), respectively. Compared to models using clinical variables alone, combining smRNAPSO, age, and clinical variables did not improve the predictive performance in the External Validation dataset (AUC 0.75 [0.71, 0.79], pвҖү=вҖү0.73). For 10вҖҗyear survival (TableВ S4), the predictive performance of smRNAs, age, clinical variables, and their combinations was even weaker, with crossвҖҗvalidated Discovery AUCs <вҖү0.7 and External Validation AUCs вүӨвҖү0.65 across all models. In short, as the prediction time horizon increased (i.e., as the time from blood acquisition to survival outcome lengthened), the predictive performance declined.

Many of the highestвҖҗperforming models included multiple variables. For example, the top 2вҖҗyear survival model (crossвҖҗvalidated Discovery AUC 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01; External Validation AUC 0.87 [0.83, 0.90]) used all 828 smRNAPSO, all 186 clinical variables, and age. To improve clinical applicability, we next sought to develop simpler, more parsimonious models that used fewer variables without compromising predictive accuracy. We therefore restricted model inputs to MB features, which theoretically achieve maximum simplicity while retaining all nonвҖҗredundant predictive information. The MBs were identified using the generalized local learning (GLL) algorithm family (Aliferis etВ al.В 2010a, 2010b) and the TIE* algorithm (Statnikov etВ al.В 2013) (see Methods S1). Using this streamlined approach, we developed a 2вҖҗyear survival model containing only six piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587552, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786, piRвҖҗDQ575235). This reduced model preserved nearly all the predictive power of the full 828 smRNA model (Discovery AUC 0.86вҖүВұвҖү0.02 vs. 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.01, pвҖү<вҖү0.01; External Validation AUC 0.83 [0.78, 0.87] vs. 0.82 [0.78, 0.87], pвҖү>вҖү0.05). When age and clinical variables were incorporated, a parsimonious model containing just 5 piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786, piRвҖҗDQ575235) and 2 clinical variablesвҖ”instrumental activities of daily living related to physical function (IADLвҖҗmotor) and total high density lipoprotein particle number (Total HDLвҖҗP)вҖ”maintained performance comparable to the full model with all 828 smRNAs, age, and all 186 clinical variables (TableВ S4). The simplified model achieved a Discovery AUC 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.02 vs. 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01 for the full model (pвҖү<вҖү0.03) and an External Validation AUC 0.85 [0.80, 0.89] vs. 0.87 [0.81, 0.90] (pвҖү>вҖү0.05). Across all time horizons, streamlined predictive models yielded performance metrics comparable to their larger counterparts (TableВ 2 and TableВ S4). Label permutation tests confirmed that our predictive modeling protocol was unbiased and statistically tested, produced signatures against the null hypothesis of no signal (TableВ S4).

The previous analysis identified MBs of survival at different time horizons. By MB theory, MBs of survival comprise the local causal pathway of survival in distributions without target information equivalence. In omics distributions, significant information equivalencies among molecules for a target phenotype (e.g., survival) often exist, where optimal and statistically equivalent information about the phenotype can be obtained from multiple sets of variables. Ignoring target information equivalence can result in false positive and false negative discovery of causal determinants (Kraus etВ al.В 2022; Simon and AliferisВ 2024; Statnikov etВ al.В 2013). To capture all statistically equivalent putative causes, we conducted MB discovery on all 1015 variables representing the combined set of smRNAs, age, and clinical features (FigureВ 2), and from smRNA data alone (FigureВ S1). From the combined set of all data, the members of the MBs identified for predicting 2вҖҗyear survival included nine piRNAs (no miRNAs), small HDL (H123) particle number, total HDL particle concentration (cHDLP), and IADLвҖҗmotor function (TableВ S3). Each MB consisted of 7вҖ“8 variables, achieving External Validation AUC 0.84 (SD 0.01) (TableВ S5). These variables are potential direct causes of 2вҖҗyear survival. Of these 9 selected piRNAs, four piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786), IADLвҖҗmotor, and HDL particle concentration (total or small) appeared in 100% of MBs (FigureВ 2, TableВ S3); given causal sufficiency (i.e., lack of unmeasured causal confounding), these variables in the intersection of all MBs identified by the algorithm are expected to be direct causes of 2вҖҗyear survival. Of the nine piRNAs selected as predictors of 2вҖҗyear survival, four piRNAs (DQ573780, DQ587354, DQ593963, and DQ597786) not in the intersection of all MBs, were also potential direct causes of 5вҖҗyear survival, with the remainder of the direct causes guaranteed to be in this set plus false positives. All nine piRNAs were consistently lower in the longвҖҗlived compared to shortвҖҗlived individuals (TableВ S3); this was an a priori unexpected finding, given the established role of piRNAs in suppressing transposon activity in the germline (Siomi etВ al.В 2011) and the doubleвҖҗstranded DNA breaks generated by transposition, that are believed to serve as a mechanism to regulate the pace of aging (Lenart etВ al.В 2018).

Markov Boundaries of longevity. (A) The most critical predictors of longevity across different time horizons, called Markov Boundary (MB) variables, were identified by machine learning. The MB variables are also potential direct causes given that longevity is a terminal variable under causal sufficiency. We estimated their ranges of estimated effect sizes for predicting 2вҖҗyear (top panel), 5вҖҗyear (middle panel) and 10вҖҗyear (lower panel) longevity. All molecular variables were derived from measures at the time of the blood drawвҖ”the third inвҖҗperson evaluation (P3). Green indicates variables that appeared in all MBs. The total sample size nвҖү=вҖү707. The box in the figure represents the range of the estimates for each variable from models with the variable present. The left (vertical) line of the box represents the 1st quantile (Q1), the right line of the box represents the 3rd quantile (Q3), the internal line represents the median, the left whiskerвҖү=вҖүmin(max(x), Q1вҖү+вҖү1.5вҖүГ—вҖүIQR), the right whisker = max(min(x), Q3вҖүвҖ“вҖү1.5вҖүГ—вҖүIQR), where IQR is the interquartile range. The large coefficient of miRвҖҗ125aвҖҗ3p is a result of its absence in all participants who survived beyond 10вҖүyears. Results are generated using smRNAPSO, that is, the platform specific outputs. (B) Heatmap illustrating the empirical distribution of the MB variables (rows) of 2вҖҗyear longevity over all participants (columns), stratified by longevity вүҘ or <вҖү2вҖүyears. Variables are ranked by effect sizes from negative to positive. Values of the variables were converted to percentiles for illustration purposes. Percentile numbers were represented with different colors (see color bar on the right). Variables names marked in green appears in all MBs. The variables with names marked in red contain the same information about 2вҖҗyear longevity given other variables in the MB. The two variables marked with * contain the same information as all other variables that are marked in blue. IADLвҖҗphysicalвҖҗP3 is instrumental activities of daily living motor function at the inвҖҗperson visit of the blood draw.

As we previously observed (Kraus etВ al.В 2022), better motor function (IADLвҖҗmotor) and higher HDL particle (total and small) counts were estimated to positively impact survival, appearing in all MBs. The strongest predictors of 2вҖҗyear survival were piRвҖҗDQ593963 and IADLвҖҗmotor, with average standardized effect estimates (sES) in the logistic regression model (see details on causal effect estimation in SectionВ 3) of вҲ’0.752 and +0.754, respectively (FigureВ 2, TableВ S6a). Notably, among the measured variables, age at study baseline did not emerge as a direct cause of 2вҖҗyear survival after blood acquisition (8вҖүyears after baseline). This suggests that once the relevant molecular factors are identified, ageвҖ”primarily serving as a proxy for these factorsвҖ”did not independently influence shortвҖҗterm survival (see the SectionВ 1.4.4 for further details).

Since smRNAs provide an aging biomarker that is potentially modifiable, we estimated the degree of improvement in survival by a theoretical (in silico) вҖңinterventionвҖқ consisting of adjusting the values of the smRNA variables identified as potential direct causes of survival for each participant to that of the 5th percentile (if the variable was estimated to hamper survival) or 95th percentile (if the variable was estimated to promote survival) (TableВ S7, right panel). The estimated effect of this вҖңinterventionвҖқ was improved probability of 2вҖҗyear survival from 47% to 90% in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 73% to 94% in the External Validation dataset. In relative terms, these represent approximately 1.9вҖҗfold and 1.3вҖҗfold increases in survival probability. Expressed inversely, the corresponding 2вҖҗyear probability of death declined from 53% to 10% (a 5.3вҖҗfold reduction) in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 27% to 6% (a 4.5вҖҗfold reduction) in the External Validation dataset.

When all potential direct causes were jointly modifiedвҖ”representing a hypothetical combined lifestyle and pharmaceutical interventionвҖ”the probability of 2вҖҗyear survival increased from 47% to 99.6% in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 73% to 99.6% in the External Validation dataset (2.1вҖҗfold and 1.4вҖҗfold increases in survival, respectively). Correspondingly, the probability of death was reduced from 53% to 0.4% (a 133вҖҗfold reduction) in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 27% to 0.4% (a 68вҖҗfold reduction) in the External Validation dataset (TableВ S7, left panel). These results underscore the key role of these causal factorsвҖ”particularly smRNAsвҖ”in 2вҖҗyear survival.

The set of equivalent MBs predicting 5вҖҗyear survival consisted of 115 MBs. Each MB contained 13вҖ“15 variables, achieving AUC 0.73вҖүВұвҖү0.01 in the independent External Validation dataset (TableВ S5, FigureВ 2, and TableВ S6b). Seven smRNAs (2 miRNAs and 5 piRNAs) were identified as potential direct causes for 5вҖҗyear survival. These findings further support a distinct causal role of piRNAs and miRNAs in survival. Lesser need for toileting assistance, higher miRвҖҗ153вҖҗ3p and serum CO2 levels, modest BMI increase, and absence of cognitive impairment were consistently associated with greater 5вҖҗyear survival, while age remained a key but indirect proxy for unmeasured or timeвҖҗvarying molecular factors. A modelвҖҗbased вҖңinterventionвҖқ on MB smRNAs alone in the independent External Validation cohort corresponded to an increase in the estimated 5вҖҗyear survival probability among nonвҖҗsurvivors from 61% to 73% (TableВ S7), corresponding to a 1.2вҖҗfold improvement in survival. Expressed inversely, the corresponding probability of death declined from 39% to 27%, representing a 1.4вҖҗfold reduction in 5вҖҗyear mortality.

Ten MBs predicted 10вҖҗyear survival equally well though with lower predictive performance compared to models for 2вҖҗ and 5вҖҗyear survival. Each MB consisted of 7вҖ“9 variables, achieving AUC 0.61вҖүВұвҖү0.01 in the External Validation dataset (TableВ S5, FigureВ 2, and TableВ S6c). Both piRNAs (3 total) and miRNAs (10 total) were identified as potential causes, but none overlapped with those that predicted 2вҖҗ and 5вҖҗyear survival. Physical function also remained a key potential cause (TableВ S6c). A modelвҖҗbased вҖңinterventionвҖқ on MB smRNAs in the independent External Validation cohort increased the estimated 10вҖҗyear survival probability from 25% to 77% (TableВ S7), corresponding to a 3.1вҖҗfold improvement in longвҖҗterm survival. The associated 10вҖҗyear probability of death declined from 75% to 23%, a 3.3вҖҗfold reduction in mortality.

To align with clinical practice, which often relies on bloodвҖҗbased biomarkers, we generated MBs using smRNA data alone, excluding clinical data and age. For predicting 2вҖҗyear survival, smRNAвҖҗderived MBs were highly robust. Three MBs were discovered, wherein each MB contained 6 smRNAs for a total of eight smRNAs overall; all MB smRNAs were piRNAsвҖ”seven negatively and one positively associated with 2вҖҗyear survival. These MBs achieved AUC 0.81вҖүВұвҖү0.02 on the External Validation dataset (TableВ S5, TableВ S6a right panel, FigureВ S1). The predictive performance of the smRNAвҖҗderived MBs declined considerably for the longer time horizons compared to the combination of smRNAs, clinical variables, and age. For 5вҖҗyear survival, 18 MBs were identified consisting of 22 smRNAs (5 of which were piRNAs) yielding External Validation AUC 0.56вҖүВұвҖү0.04; for 10вҖҗyear survival, 3 MBs were identified consisting of 5 smRNAs (one of which was a piRNA) yielding External Validation AUC 0.55вҖүВұвҖүSD 0.01 (TablesВ S5 and S6b,c right panels, FigureВ S1). These findings underscore the strong shortвҖҗterm predictive power of piRNAs and suggest that additional factors are required for accurate longвҖҗterm survival predictions.

A вҖңsepsetвҖқ is a set of variables that causally mediates (or confounds) the effect (or association, respectively) of another variable on the outcome (survival), leading to the latter's exclusion from the MBs. Sepset analysis describes why particular variables are not selected in the MBs for survival, and therefore, are not considered potential direct causal factors for survival, even if they are correlated with it. Sepset analysis can help bring clarity to results within a study and across studies. For example, the total number of HDL particles was identified as a sepset for age, indicating that HDL particle levels encapsulated all 2вҖҗyear survivalвҖҗrelated information attributable to age (TableВ S8). Similarly, piRвҖҗDQ593963 acted as a sepset for sex, while IADLвҖҗmotor (FillenbaumВ 1988; Lawton and BrodyВ 1969) was a sepset for other functional measures, such as RosowвҖҗBreslau mobility (Rosow and BreslauВ 1966), NagiвҖҗmodified IADL and motor activity (NagiВ 1976), and Katz basic activities of daily living (Katz etВ al.В 1970). At the 2вҖҗyear time horizon, piRвҖҗDQ593963 was the most frequently identified sepset.

At the 5вҖҗyear time horizon, miRвҖҗ153вҖҗ3p rendered most of the smRNAs irrelevant for 5вҖҗyear survival. piRвҖҗDQ573780 and piRвҖҗDQ593963 served as sepsets for the remaining smRNAs. Age was itself a sepset for several clinical factors, including serum protein, lipoprotein insulin resistance (LPIR), red blood cell count, hemoglobin, hematocrit, income, depression, and rarely feeling rested upon awakening (SLEEP5).

Because multiple databases use distinct naming conventions for piRNAs, TableВ S9 lists all identified piRNAs with their corresponding accession IDs, annotations across major databases (NCBI, piRNAQuest v2, piRBase, and piRNA_DB), genomic loci, sequence length, and nucleotide sequence and predicted gene targets; miRNAs are similarly annotated with their miRBase IDs and corresponding gene targets. Ten piRNAs predicting 2вҖҗyear survival targeted 65 mRNA transcripts, including four pseudogenes in addition to the Long Interspersed Nuclear ElementвҖҗ1 (LINEвҖҗ1) retrotransposon (TableВ S9). The seven piRNAs predicting 5вҖҗyear survival targeted 19 mRNA transcripts, while the three piRNAs predicting 10вҖҗyear survival targeted eight mRNA transcripts and several miRNAs, notably the miRвҖҗ17вҖҗ92 cluster, with key roles in cell proliferation and survival, immune regulation, apoptosis, and oncogenesis (Grillari etВ al.В 2010). No miRNAs were identified as predictors of 2вҖҗyear survival, while 18 miRNAs predicted 5вҖҗyear survival; among these, two were members of the miRвҖҗ17вҖҗ92 cluster and one a member of the miRвҖҗ106aвҖҗ363 paralogous cluster of the miRвҖҗ17вҖҗ92 cluster.

Using the interactive mSALT database (Tyshkovskiy etВ al.В 2023)вҖ”a platform for visualizing associations between mammalian gene expression and aging or longevity traitsвҖ”we expanded the biological understanding of four mRNA targets corresponding to three of the four smRNAs that appeared in 100% of MBs for this trait and were therefore identified as estimated direct causal drivers of 2вҖҗyear survival (FigureВ S2). Macro H2A.1 Histone (MACRO2A1, a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ573780), a gene involved in chromosomal stability, transcriptional regulation, DNA repair and replication (HernГЎndezвҖҗMuГұoz etВ al.В 2005), is unchanged with aging in humans but consistently down regulated by longevityвҖҗpromoting interventions such as calorie restriction (CR), growth hormone deficiency, rapamycin, and other common proвҖҗlongevity interventions. Potassium voltageвҖҗgated channel interacting protein 4 (KCNIP4, also a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ573780), which modulates neuronal excitability through regulation of potassium channels (Pruunsild and TimmuskВ 2005), shows a nonвҖҗsignificant decrease with age in humans. WEE2 antisense RNA 1 (WEE2вҖҗAS1, a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ593963) is a long nonвҖҗcoding RNA (lncRNA) involved in meiotic cell division (Hanna etВ al.В 2010), is reduced by CR and common proвҖҗlongevity interventions. Solute carrier family 9вҖҗmember a7 (SLC9A7, a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ597786), involved in regulation of cellular pH balance (Kagami etВ al.В 2008), decreases with aging in humans and increases with CR. These expression patterns across species and interventions underscore the biological relevance of these smRNA targets in modulating lifespan.

Using Database for Annotation, Visualization, and Integrated Discovery (DAVID), we identified significant pathways through functional enrichment analysis of mRNAs predicted by survivalвҖҗassociated smRNAs (TableВ S10); these included a longevityвҖҗregulating pathway (Bonferroni pвҖү=вҖү0.0001). The overall predicted targets were enriched for transcriptional, cellвҖҗcycle, and stress/inflammatory signaling pathwaysвҖ”including MAPK, PI3KвҖ“Akt, TP53, and TLR cascadesвҖ”indicating convergence on regulatory programs that govern proliferation, apoptosis, and cellular stress responses. Additional pathway analysis focused on the 5вҖҗyear survival predictors because this time horizon included a sufficient number of identified miRNAs and could be analyzed using available tools such as MiRTarBase 9.0 for mRNA target prediction. After filtering for вҖңstrong evidenceвҖқвҖ”defined by miRTarBase as interactions validated by direct experimental assays (e.g., reporter, Western blot, or qPCR; see Methods S1)вҖ”we identified 2233 mRNAs targeted by the 18 miRNAs, with each miRNA predicted to target 12вҖ“441 mRNAs. Notably, 477 mRNAs were targeted by multiple miRNAs (FigureВ 3A). STRING network analysis (Szklarczyk etВ al.В 2019) revealed that these shared targets were highly interactive and enriched in 79 Reactome pathways. Four of the 79 pathways were senescenceвҖҗrelated; two of these, cellular senescence and oxidative stressвҖҗinduced senescence, were among the top 10 pathways (FigureВ 3B). Six pathways were apoptosisвҖҗrelated, three stressвҖҗresponseвҖҗrelated, and four immune systemвҖҗrelated (FigureВ 3C). Additionally, many targets were implicated in regulating biosynthetic processes, mitochondrial function, metabolic processes and cell death (FigureВ S3).

The bar graph displays the number of mRNA targets of the individual miRNAs, totaling 2233 mRNAs; among which 477 mRNAs were targets of multiple miRNAs. (B, C) Search Tool for the Retrieval of Interacting Genes/Proteins (STRING) network analyses were used to identify the significantly enriched (False Discovery Rate [FDR]вҖү<вҖү0.05) Reactome Pathways of 474 targets; 3 targets (AHSA2, AZF1, and TSPEARвҖҗAS2) were not able to be mapped. Our analyses are based on 1271 participants from the communityвҖҗbased DukeвҖҗEstablished Populations for Epidemiologic Studies of the Elderly (DвҖҗEPESE) cohort, all aged вүҘвҖү71вҖүyears at the time of blood sample collection in year six of the study (1992вҖ“93). In brief, DвҖҗEPESE was a multiвҖҗyear study sponsored by the National Institute on Aging to describe and identify predictors of mortality and investigate risk factors for chronic diseases and loss of functioning (Blazer and GeorgeВ 2012). The North Carolina cohort, established in 1986, was a sample of persons 65вҖүyears or older residing in households in five counties in the Central Piedmont area. This unique NC cohort, with over 50% Black participants at baseline and drawn from a region encompassing diverse racial and urbanвҖ“rural populations, enabled comparisons of risk factors and disease distribution across these groups.

Biomarker Discovery and Internal Validation

To discover and validate plasma smRNAs (piRNAs and miRNAs) with predictive and causal relevance for survival, we employed a rigorous, multiвҖҗstage protocol to ensure unbiased model selection, error estimation, and generalizability (FigureВ 1). We used nextвҖҗgeneration sequencing to quantify smRNA levels in 707 participants from the DвҖҗEPESE cohort (Kraus etВ al.В 2022). This вҖңExpanded DiscoveryвҖқ dataset comprised a random half of the DвҖҗEPESE cohort with available plasma biospecimens and, was originally selected using stratified randomization to ensure balance by race, sex, age, and survival status (Methods S1). In the first stage of discovery and validation, the Expanded Discovery dataset was further randomly divided into a вҖңDiscovery SubsetвҖқ dataset (nвҖү=вҖү505) and an вҖңInternal ValidationвҖқ dataset (nвҖү=вҖү202) (FigureВ 1). All data splits preserved the proportion of surviving and nonвҖҗsurviving participants across both the inner and outer loops (see SectionВ 3). Using the Discovery Subset dataset, we built predictive models incorporating 828 smRNAs, age, or 186 clinical features, and their combinations, to predict survival across 2вҖҗ, 5вҖҗ, and 10вҖҗyear intervals following the timepoint of plasma acquisition used for profiling smRNA. The 186 clinical features consisted of sociodemographic variables including demographics and anthropometric measures (age, race, sex, education, income, body mass index [BMI]), medical morbidities, selfвҖҗrated health, depression, health behaviors (smoking habits, alcohol use, sleep), cognitive and physical/motor activity status, and soluble analytes including 48 traditional medical blood tests, 6 additional inflammation parameters, and 48 lipoprotein biomarkers (NMR LipoProfile blood test) (Kraus etВ al.В 2022).

Analytical study design. (A) Leveraging molecular and clinical data from 1271 participants in the DвҖҗEPESE study, we built models to identify predictors and causes of survival at different time horizons (2вҖҗ, 5вҖҗ, and 10вҖҗyears) following best practices for complex statistical machine learning modeling to ensure unbiased and generalizable estimation of predictive performance and causal relevance. The Expanded Discovery dataset (NвҖү=вҖү707; smRNA sequenced in one batch) was split into a Discovery Subset (NвҖү=вҖү505) and an Internal Validation set (NвҖү=вҖү202), while an independent External Validation set (NвҖү=вҖү564), drawn from a separately withheld half of the DвҖҗEPESE cohort and sequenced in a different batch, was used to evaluate model performance, generalizability, and robustness to smRNA measurement variability. (B) Summary of the analytical design. Symbols in blue denote predictive modeling analyses and in green denote causal modeling analyses. Predictive Modeling was conducted to build and validate predictive models for longevity based on smRNA with and without other patient characteristics. For the first stage model discovery and internal validation, we aimed to establish internal validity of our models given smRNA data measured within the same assay batch. We used the Discovery Subset to build predictive models and conduct model selection (inner loop of the stratified repeated nested crossвҖҗvalidation [NCV]). For the second stage of model discovery, we aimed to establish external validity of our models, specifically, the generalizability of our models across different (nonвҖҗoverlapping) batches of biological samples and assays. We used the Expanded Discovery data for model selection and performance estimation via NCV and built the final predictive model (with the best hyperparameters identified), and then subsequently and independently tested the model on independent External Validation data (NвҖү=вҖү564) where smRNAs were sequenced as a separate batch. A label permutation test was conducted to assess potential error estimation biases. Causal Modeling was conducted to identify potential proximal causes (local pathways) for survival using the Expanded Discovery data with Markov Boundary analysis. The causal effects of the identified causes were also estimated. Sepset analyses were conducted to gain more biological insights into the causal role of the study variables. These results were crossвҖҗchecked with prior findings from the literature. Panel A created in BioRender, agreement FD287GWPQ8.

Models were trained on the Discovery Subset dataset (nвҖү=вҖү505), with unbiased performance estimates obtained from the outer loop of nested crossвҖҗvalidation (NCV) and from the Internal Validation dataset (nвҖү=вҖү202). The smRNAвҖҗonly model for 2вҖҗyear survival achieved crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.89вҖүВұвҖү0.02 in the Discovery Subset and validation AUC 0.91 (95% CI: 0.84вҖ“0.98) in the independent Internal Validation dataset (TableВ S1), demonstrating strong predictive performance of smRNAs for 2вҖҗyear survival in the Expanded Discovery data overall. The smRNAвҖҗonly models demonstrated lower accuracy for 5вҖҗ and 10вҖҗyear predictions, yielding AUCs 0.63 and 0.65, respectively, in the independent Internal Validation dataset (TableВ S1). Models incorporating smRNAs, age, and clinical features improved predictive accuracy for 5вҖҗyear survival (AUC 0.77) but showed limited enhancement of 10вҖҗyear predictions (AUC 0.66). These findings highlight the strong shortвҖҗterm predictive value of smRNAs while emphasizing the need for integrated models to improve survival predictions over extended timeframes. Additional predictive performance details for other models and combinations can be found in TableВ S1.

As part of the discovery processвҖ”and to simplify clinical testing for optimal quality and consistencyвҖ”we compared the performance of the initial Qiagen platformвҖҗspecific output (smRNAPSO) with data normalized using the standard miRNA normalization method, smRNATMM (normalized Trimmed Mean of MвҖҗvalues [TMM values]) (Robinson and OshlackВ 2010). Across both the Discovery Subset and Internal Validation datasets, smRNAPSO and smRNATMM demonstrated statistically comparable predictive performance (pвҖү>вҖү0.05) for most outcomes (TableВ S2). Given its simplicity and greater practicality for clinical use, we selected smRNAPSO for all subsequent analyses to streamline future clinical applications.

External Validation in an Independent Cohort With Separate

To strengthen bias prevention and to assess generalizability associated with sequencing batch effects, a doubleвҖҗholdout design was instituted (FigureВ 1). In addition to the nested cross validation followed by internal validation described above, an external validation stage was implemented. To create a separate group of individuals, distinct from the data used to build the model to assess the model's performance, generalizability, and robustness to variability in smRNA measurements, we used an independent sample of 564 participants from DвҖҗEPESE. These individuals were drawn from the remaining randomly withheld half of the DвҖҗEPESE cohort; the plasmas of the independent External Validation cohort were processed and sequenced for smRNA in a separate batch, using the same methods as for the Expanded Discovery dataset but with a slightly greater depth of sequencing (see SectionВ 3). Descriptive statistics in TableВ 1 present clinical features for each DвҖҗEPESE sample dataset; descriptive statistics in TableВ S3 present clinical features and smRNAs of Markov Boundaries (MBs, i.e., from causal analysis) for the Expanded Discovery dataset by survival status (alive/dead) for each time horizon. Methods followed established best practices (Aliferis and SimonВ 2024a, 2024c) (see SectionВ 3 and Methods S1), including an outcome label permutation test (Ojala and GarrigaВ 2010) (FigureВ 1, TableВ S4) to confirm unbiasedness. This comprehensive protocolвҖ”integrating model selection and fitting, error estimation, bias avoidance, and causal interpretationвҖ”validated smRNAs as robust and clinically useful biomarkers of survival. Results are described below for the Expanded Discovery cohort (nвҖү=вҖү707) and the independent External Validation cohort (nвҖү=вҖү564).

For 2вҖҗyear survival (TableВ 2 and TableВ S4 with full details), the predictive model built with smRNAPSO data achieved nested crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.01 in the Expanded Discovery dataset (nвҖү=вҖү707) and AUC 0.82 (0.78, 0.87) in the independent External Validation dataset (nвҖү=вҖү564) that underwent smRNA sequencing in a separate batch. The predictive performance of smRNAPSO was significantly better (pвҖү<вҖү0.05) than models built with age or clinical variables alone: age crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.57вҖүВұвҖү0.03 and External Validation AUC 0.56 (0.50, 0.62); and clinical variables crossвҖҗvalidated AUC 0.79вҖүВұвҖү0.02 and External Validation AUC 0.74 (0.69, 0.80). Compared with models using smRNAPSO alone, combining smRNAPSO, age, and clinical variables resulted in improved predictive performance in the crossвҖҗvalidated Discovery (AUC 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01, pвҖү=вҖү0.03), but comparable performance in the External Validation dataset (AUC 0.87 [0.83, 0.90], pвҖү=вҖү0.35).

For 5вҖҗyear survival (TableВ S4), most models showed lower predictive performance than for 2вҖҗyear survival. Models with smRNAPSO, age, or clinical variables achieved independent External Validation AUCs 0.61 (0.57, 0.66), 0.64 (0.59, 0.68), and 0.74 (0.68, 0.77), respectively. Compared to models using clinical variables alone, combining smRNAPSO, age, and clinical variables did not improve the predictive performance in the External Validation dataset (AUC 0.75 [0.71, 0.79], pвҖү=вҖү0.73). For 10вҖҗyear survival (TableВ S4), the predictive performance of smRNAs, age, clinical variables, and their combinations was even weaker, with crossвҖҗvalidated Discovery AUCs <вҖү0.7 and External Validation AUCs вүӨвҖү0.65 across all models. In short, as the prediction time horizon increased (i.e., as the time from blood acquisition to survival outcome lengthened), the predictive performance declined.

Many of the highestвҖҗperforming models included multiple variables. For example, the top 2вҖҗyear survival model (crossвҖҗvalidated Discovery AUC 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01; External Validation AUC 0.87 [0.83, 0.90]) used all 828 smRNAPSO, all 186 clinical variables, and age. To improve clinical applicability, we next sought to develop simpler, more parsimonious models that used fewer variables without compromising predictive accuracy. We therefore restricted model inputs to MB features, which theoretically achieve maximum simplicity while retaining all nonвҖҗredundant predictive information. The MBs were identified using the generalized local learning (GLL) algorithm family (Aliferis etВ al.В 2010a, 2010b) and the TIE* algorithm (Statnikov etВ al.В 2013) (see Methods S1). Using this streamlined approach, we developed a 2вҖҗyear survival model containing only six piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587552, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786, piRвҖҗDQ575235). This reduced model preserved nearly all the predictive power of the full 828 smRNA model (Discovery AUC 0.86вҖүВұвҖү0.02 vs. 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.01, pвҖү<вҖү0.01; External Validation AUC 0.83 [0.78, 0.87] vs. 0.82 [0.78, 0.87], pвҖү>вҖү0.05). When age and clinical variables were incorporated, a parsimonious model containing just 5 piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786, piRвҖҗDQ575235) and 2 clinical variablesвҖ”instrumental activities of daily living related to physical function (IADLвҖҗmotor) and total high density lipoprotein particle number (Total HDLвҖҗP)вҖ”maintained performance comparable to the full model with all 828 smRNAs, age, and all 186 clinical variables (TableВ S4). The simplified model achieved a Discovery AUC 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.02 vs. 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01 for the full model (pвҖү<вҖү0.03) and an External Validation AUC 0.85 [0.80, 0.89] vs. 0.87 [0.81, 0.90] (pвҖү>вҖү0.05). Across all time horizons, streamlined predictive models yielded performance metrics comparable to their larger counterparts (TableВ 2 and TableВ S4). Label permutation tests confirmed that our predictive modeling protocol was unbiased and statistically tested, produced signatures against the null hypothesis of no signal (TableВ S4).

Streamlined Predictive Models for Practical Clinical Deployment

Many of the highestвҖҗperforming models included multiple variables. For example, the top 2вҖҗyear survival model (crossвҖҗvalidated Discovery AUC 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01; External Validation AUC 0.87 [0.83, 0.90]) used all 828 smRNAPSO, all 186 clinical variables, and age. To improve clinical applicability, we next sought to develop simpler, more parsimonious models that used fewer variables without compromising predictive accuracy. We therefore restricted model inputs to MB features, which theoretically achieve maximum simplicity while retaining all nonвҖҗredundant predictive information. The MBs were identified using the generalized local learning (GLL) algorithm family (Aliferis etВ al.В 2010a, 2010b) and the TIE* algorithm (Statnikov etВ al.В 2013) (see Methods S1). Using this streamlined approach, we developed a 2вҖҗyear survival model containing only six piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587552, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786, piRвҖҗDQ575235). This reduced model preserved nearly all the predictive power of the full 828 smRNA model (Discovery AUC 0.86вҖүВұвҖү0.02 vs. 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.01, pвҖү<вҖү0.01; External Validation AUC 0.83 [0.78, 0.87] vs. 0.82 [0.78, 0.87], pвҖү>вҖү0.05). When age and clinical variables were incorporated, a parsimonious model containing just 5 piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786, piRвҖҗDQ575235) and 2 clinical variablesвҖ”instrumental activities of daily living related to physical function (IADLвҖҗmotor) and total high density lipoprotein particle number (Total HDLвҖҗP)вҖ”maintained performance comparable to the full model with all 828 smRNAs, age, and all 186 clinical variables (TableВ S4). The simplified model achieved a Discovery AUC 0.90вҖүВұвҖү0.02 vs. 0.92вҖүВұвҖү0.01 for the full model (pвҖү<вҖү0.03) and an External Validation AUC 0.85 [0.80, 0.89] vs. 0.87 [0.81, 0.90] (pвҖү>вҖү0.05). Across all time horizons, streamlined predictive models yielded performance metrics comparable to their larger counterparts (TableВ 2 and TableВ S4). Label permutation tests confirmed that our predictive modeling protocol was unbiased and statistically tested, produced signatures against the null hypothesis of no signal (TableВ S4).

Algorithmic Discovery of Markov Boundaries and Potential Direct Causes of Survival Across Time Horizons

The previous analysis identified MBs of survival at different time horizons. By MB theory, MBs of survival comprise the local causal pathway of survival in distributions without target information equivalence. In omics distributions, significant information equivalencies among molecules for a target phenotype (e.g., survival) often exist, where optimal and statistically equivalent information about the phenotype can be obtained from multiple sets of variables. Ignoring target information equivalence can result in false positive and false negative discovery of causal determinants (Kraus etВ al.В 2022; Simon and AliferisВ 2024; Statnikov etВ al.В 2013). To capture all statistically equivalent putative causes, we conducted MB discovery on all 1015 variables representing the combined set of smRNAs, age, and clinical features (FigureВ 2), and from smRNA data alone (FigureВ S1). From the combined set of all data, the members of the MBs identified for predicting 2вҖҗyear survival included nine piRNAs (no miRNAs), small HDL (H123) particle number, total HDL particle concentration (cHDLP), and IADLвҖҗmotor function (TableВ S3). Each MB consisted of 7вҖ“8 variables, achieving External Validation AUC 0.84 (SD 0.01) (TableВ S5). These variables are potential direct causes of 2вҖҗyear survival. Of these 9 selected piRNAs, four piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786), IADLвҖҗmotor, and HDL particle concentration (total or small) appeared in 100% of MBs (FigureВ 2, TableВ S3); given causal sufficiency (i.e., lack of unmeasured causal confounding), these variables in the intersection of all MBs identified by the algorithm are expected to be direct causes of 2вҖҗyear survival. Of the nine piRNAs selected as predictors of 2вҖҗyear survival, four piRNAs (DQ573780, DQ587354, DQ593963, and DQ597786) not in the intersection of all MBs, were also potential direct causes of 5вҖҗyear survival, with the remainder of the direct causes guaranteed to be in this set plus false positives. All nine piRNAs were consistently lower in the longвҖҗlived compared to shortвҖҗlived individuals (TableВ S3); this was an a priori unexpected finding, given the established role of piRNAs in suppressing transposon activity in the germline (Siomi etВ al.В 2011) and the doubleвҖҗstranded DNA breaks generated by transposition, that are believed to serve as a mechanism to regulate the pace of aging (Lenart etВ al.В 2018).

As we previously observed (Kraus etВ al.В 2022), better motor function (IADLвҖҗmotor) and higher HDL particle (total and small) counts were estimated to positively impact survival, appearing in all MBs. The strongest predictors of 2вҖҗyear survival were piRвҖҗDQ593963 and IADLвҖҗmotor, with average standardized effect estimates (sES) in the logistic regression model (see details on causal effect estimation in SectionВ 3) of вҲ’0.752 and +0.754, respectively (FigureВ 2, TableВ S6a). Notably, among the measured variables, age at study baseline did not emerge as a direct cause of 2вҖҗyear survival after blood acquisition (8вҖүyears after baseline). This suggests that once the relevant molecular factors are identified, ageвҖ”primarily serving as a proxy for these factorsвҖ”did not independently influence shortвҖҗterm survival (see the SectionВ 1.4.4 for further details).

Since smRNAs provide an aging biomarker that is potentially modifiable, we estimated the degree of improvement in survival by a theoretical (in silico) вҖңinterventionвҖқ consisting of adjusting the values of the smRNA variables identified as potential direct causes of survival for each participant to that of the 5th percentile (if the variable was estimated to hamper survival) or 95th percentile (if the variable was estimated to promote survival) (TableВ S7, right panel). The estimated effect of this вҖңinterventionвҖқ was improved probability of 2вҖҗyear survival from 47% to 90% in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 73% to 94% in the External Validation dataset. In relative terms, these represent approximately 1.9вҖҗfold and 1.3вҖҗfold increases in survival probability. Expressed inversely, the corresponding 2вҖҗyear probability of death declined from 53% to 10% (a 5.3вҖҗfold reduction) in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 27% to 6% (a 4.5вҖҗfold reduction) in the External Validation dataset.

When all potential direct causes were jointly modifiedвҖ”representing a hypothetical combined lifestyle and pharmaceutical interventionвҖ”the probability of 2вҖҗyear survival increased from 47% to 99.6% in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 73% to 99.6% in the External Validation dataset (2.1вҖҗfold and 1.4вҖҗfold increases in survival, respectively). Correspondingly, the probability of death was reduced from 53% to 0.4% (a 133вҖҗfold reduction) in the Expanded Discovery dataset and from 27% to 0.4% (a 68вҖҗfold reduction) in the External Validation dataset (TableВ S7, left panel). These results underscore the key role of these causal factorsвҖ”particularly smRNAsвҖ”in 2вҖҗyear survival.

The set of equivalent MBs predicting 5вҖҗyear survival consisted of 115 MBs. Each MB contained 13вҖ“15 variables, achieving AUC 0.73вҖүВұвҖү0.01 in the independent External Validation dataset (TableВ S5, FigureВ 2, and TableВ S6b). Seven smRNAs (2 miRNAs and 5 piRNAs) were identified as potential direct causes for 5вҖҗyear survival. These findings further support a distinct causal role of piRNAs and miRNAs in survival. Lesser need for toileting assistance, higher miRвҖҗ153вҖҗ3p and serum CO2 levels, modest BMI increase, and absence of cognitive impairment were consistently associated with greater 5вҖҗyear survival, while age remained a key but indirect proxy for unmeasured or timeвҖҗvarying molecular factors. A modelвҖҗbased вҖңinterventionвҖқ on MB smRNAs alone in the independent External Validation cohort corresponded to an increase in the estimated 5вҖҗyear survival probability among nonвҖҗsurvivors from 61% to 73% (TableВ S7), corresponding to a 1.2вҖҗfold improvement in survival. Expressed inversely, the corresponding probability of death declined from 39% to 27%, representing a 1.4вҖҗfold reduction in 5вҖҗyear mortality.

Ten MBs predicted 10вҖҗyear survival equally well though with lower predictive performance compared to models for 2вҖҗ and 5вҖҗyear survival. Each MB consisted of 7вҖ“9 variables, achieving AUC 0.61вҖүВұвҖү0.01 in the External Validation dataset (TableВ S5, FigureВ 2, and TableВ S6c). Both piRNAs (3 total) and miRNAs (10 total) were identified as potential causes, but none overlapped with those that predicted 2вҖҗ and 5вҖҗyear survival. Physical function also remained a key potential cause (TableВ S6c). A modelвҖҗbased вҖңinterventionвҖқ on MB smRNAs in the independent External Validation cohort increased the estimated 10вҖҗyear survival probability from 25% to 77% (TableВ S7), corresponding to a 3.1вҖҗfold improvement in longвҖҗterm survival. The associated 10вҖҗyear probability of death declined from 75% to 23%, a 3.3вҖҗfold reduction in mortality.

To align with clinical practice, which often relies on bloodвҖҗbased biomarkers, we generated MBs using smRNA data alone, excluding clinical data and age. For predicting 2вҖҗyear survival, smRNAвҖҗderived MBs were highly robust. Three MBs were discovered, wherein each MB contained 6 smRNAs for a total of eight smRNAs overall; all MB smRNAs were piRNAsвҖ”seven negatively and one positively associated with 2вҖҗyear survival. These MBs achieved AUC 0.81вҖүВұвҖү0.02 on the External Validation dataset (TableВ S5, TableВ S6a right panel, FigureВ S1). The predictive performance of the smRNAвҖҗderived MBs declined considerably for the longer time horizons compared to the combination of smRNAs, clinical variables, and age. For 5вҖҗyear survival, 18 MBs were identified consisting of 22 smRNAs (5 of which were piRNAs) yielding External Validation AUC 0.56вҖүВұвҖү0.04; for 10вҖҗyear survival, 3 MBs were identified consisting of 5 smRNAs (one of which was a piRNA) yielding External Validation AUC 0.55вҖүВұвҖүSD 0.01 (TablesВ S5 and S6b,c right panels, FigureВ S1). These findings underscore the strong shortвҖҗterm predictive power of piRNAs and suggest that additional factors are required for accurate longвҖҗterm survival predictions.

A вҖңsepsetвҖқ is a set of variables that causally mediates (or confounds) the effect (or association, respectively) of another variable on the outcome (survival), leading to the latter's exclusion from the MBs. Sepset analysis describes why particular variables are not selected in the MBs for survival, and therefore, are not considered potential direct causal factors for survival, even if they are correlated with it. Sepset analysis can help bring clarity to results within a study and across studies. For example, the total number of HDL particles was identified as a sepset for age, indicating that HDL particle levels encapsulated all 2вҖҗyear survivalвҖҗrelated information attributable to age (TableВ S8). Similarly, piRвҖҗDQ593963 acted as a sepset for sex, while IADLвҖҗmotor (FillenbaumВ 1988; Lawton and BrodyВ 1969) was a sepset for other functional measures, such as RosowвҖҗBreslau mobility (Rosow and BreslauВ 1966), NagiвҖҗmodified IADL and motor activity (NagiВ 1976), and Katz basic activities of daily living (Katz etВ al.В 1970). At the 2вҖҗyear time horizon, piRвҖҗDQ593963 was the most frequently identified sepset.

At the 5вҖҗyear time horizon, miRвҖҗ153вҖҗ3p rendered most of the smRNAs irrelevant for 5вҖҗyear survival. piRвҖҗDQ573780 and piRвҖҗDQ593963 served as sepsets for the remaining smRNAs. Age was itself a sepset for several clinical factors, including serum protein, lipoprotein insulin resistance (LPIR), red blood cell count, hemoglobin, hematocrit, income, depression, and rarely feeling rested upon awakening (SLEEP5).

Identification of

The previous analysis identified MBs of survival at different time horizons. By MB theory, MBs of survival comprise the local causal pathway of survival in distributions without target information equivalence. In omics distributions, significant information equivalencies among molecules for a target phenotype (e.g., survival) often exist, where optimal and statistically equivalent information about the phenotype can be obtained from multiple sets of variables. Ignoring target information equivalence can result in false positive and false negative discovery of causal determinants (Kraus etВ al.В 2022; Simon and AliferisВ 2024; Statnikov etВ al.В 2013). To capture all statistically equivalent putative causes, we conducted MB discovery on all 1015 variables representing the combined set of smRNAs, age, and clinical features (FigureВ 2), and from smRNA data alone (FigureВ S1). From the combined set of all data, the members of the MBs identified for predicting 2вҖҗyear survival included nine piRNAs (no miRNAs), small HDL (H123) particle number, total HDL particle concentration (cHDLP), and IADLвҖҗmotor function (TableВ S3). Each MB consisted of 7вҖ“8 variables, achieving External Validation AUC 0.84 (SD 0.01) (TableВ S5). These variables are potential direct causes of 2вҖҗyear survival. Of these 9 selected piRNAs, four piRNAs (piRвҖҗDQ573780, piRвҖҗDQ587821, piRвҖҗDQ593963, piRвҖҗDQ597786), IADLвҖҗmotor, and HDL particle concentration (total or small) appeared in 100% of MBs (FigureВ 2, TableВ S3); given causal sufficiency (i.e., lack of unmeasured causal confounding), these variables in the intersection of all MBs identified by the algorithm are expected to be direct causes of 2вҖҗyear survival. Of the nine piRNAs selected as predictors of 2вҖҗyear survival, four piRNAs (DQ573780, DQ587354, DQ593963, and DQ597786) not in the intersection of all MBs, were also potential direct causes of 5вҖҗyear survival, with the remainder of the direct causes guaranteed to be in this set plus false positives. All nine piRNAs were consistently lower in the longвҖҗlived compared to shortвҖҗlived individuals (TableВ S3); this was an a priori unexpected finding, given the established role of piRNAs in suppressing transposon activity in the germline (Siomi etВ al.В 2011) and the doubleвҖҗstranded DNA breaks generated by transposition, that are believed to serve as a mechanism to regulate the pace of aging (Lenart etВ al.В 2018).

To align with clinical practice, which often relies on bloodвҖҗbased biomarkers, we generated MBs using smRNA data alone, excluding clinical data and age. For predicting 2вҖҗyear survival, smRNAвҖҗderived MBs were highly robust. Three MBs were discovered, wherein each MB contained 6 smRNAs for a total of eight smRNAs overall; all MB smRNAs were piRNAsвҖ”seven negatively and one positively associated with 2вҖҗyear survival. These MBs achieved AUC 0.81вҖүВұвҖү0.02 on the External Validation dataset (TableВ S5, TableВ S6a right panel, FigureВ S1). The predictive performance of the smRNAвҖҗderived MBs declined considerably for the longer time horizons compared to the combination of smRNAs, clinical variables, and age. For 5вҖҗyear survival, 18 MBs were identified consisting of 22 smRNAs (5 of which were piRNAs) yielding External Validation AUC 0.56вҖүВұвҖү0.04; for 10вҖҗyear survival, 3 MBs were identified consisting of 5 smRNAs (one of which was a piRNA) yielding External Validation AUC 0.55вҖүВұвҖүSD 0.01 (TablesВ S5 and S6b,c right panels, FigureВ S1). These findings underscore the strong shortвҖҗterm predictive power of piRNAs and suggest that additional factors are required for accurate longвҖҗterm survival predictions.

Sepset Analyses

A вҖңsepsetвҖқ is a set of variables that causally mediates (or confounds) the effect (or association, respectively) of another variable on the outcome (survival), leading to the latter's exclusion from the MBs. Sepset analysis describes why particular variables are not selected in the MBs for survival, and therefore, are not considered potential direct causal factors for survival, even if they are correlated with it. Sepset analysis can help bring clarity to results within a study and across studies. For example, the total number of HDL particles was identified as a sepset for age, indicating that HDL particle levels encapsulated all 2вҖҗyear survivalвҖҗrelated information attributable to age (TableВ S8). Similarly, piRвҖҗDQ593963 acted as a sepset for sex, while IADLвҖҗmotor (FillenbaumВ 1988; Lawton and BrodyВ 1969) was a sepset for other functional measures, such as RosowвҖҗBreslau mobility (Rosow and BreslauВ 1966), NagiвҖҗmodified IADL and motor activity (NagiВ 1976), and Katz basic activities of daily living (Katz etВ al.В 1970). At the 2вҖҗyear time horizon, piRвҖҗDQ593963 was the most frequently identified sepset.

At the 5вҖҗyear time horizon, miRвҖҗ153вҖҗ3p rendered most of the smRNAs irrelevant for 5вҖҗyear survival. piRвҖҗDQ573780 and piRвҖҗDQ593963 served as sepsets for the remaining smRNAs. Age was itself a sepset for several clinical factors, including serum protein, lipoprotein insulin resistance (LPIR), red blood cell count, hemoglobin, hematocrit, income, depression, and rarely feeling rested upon awakening (SLEEP5).

Identification of Putative

Because multiple databases use distinct naming conventions for piRNAs, TableВ S9 lists all identified piRNAs with their corresponding accession IDs, annotations across major databases (NCBI, piRNAQuest v2, piRBase, and piRNA_DB), genomic loci, sequence length, and nucleotide sequence and predicted gene targets; miRNAs are similarly annotated with their miRBase IDs and corresponding gene targets. Ten piRNAs predicting 2вҖҗyear survival targeted 65 mRNA transcripts, including four pseudogenes in addition to the Long Interspersed Nuclear ElementвҖҗ1 (LINEвҖҗ1) retrotransposon (TableВ S9). The seven piRNAs predicting 5вҖҗyear survival targeted 19 mRNA transcripts, while the three piRNAs predicting 10вҖҗyear survival targeted eight mRNA transcripts and several miRNAs, notably the miRвҖҗ17вҖҗ92 cluster, with key roles in cell proliferation and survival, immune regulation, apoptosis, and oncogenesis (Grillari etВ al.В 2010). No miRNAs were identified as predictors of 2вҖҗyear survival, while 18 miRNAs predicted 5вҖҗyear survival; among these, two were members of the miRвҖҗ17вҖҗ92 cluster and one a member of the miRвҖҗ106aвҖҗ363 paralogous cluster of the miRвҖҗ17вҖҗ92 cluster.

Using the interactive mSALT database (Tyshkovskiy etВ al.В 2023)вҖ”a platform for visualizing associations between mammalian gene expression and aging or longevity traitsвҖ”we expanded the biological understanding of four mRNA targets corresponding to three of the four smRNAs that appeared in 100% of MBs for this trait and were therefore identified as estimated direct causal drivers of 2вҖҗyear survival (FigureВ S2). Macro H2A.1 Histone (MACRO2A1, a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ573780), a gene involved in chromosomal stability, transcriptional regulation, DNA repair and replication (HernГЎndezвҖҗMuГұoz etВ al.В 2005), is unchanged with aging in humans but consistently down regulated by longevityвҖҗpromoting interventions such as calorie restriction (CR), growth hormone deficiency, rapamycin, and other common proвҖҗlongevity interventions. Potassium voltageвҖҗgated channel interacting protein 4 (KCNIP4, also a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ573780), which modulates neuronal excitability through regulation of potassium channels (Pruunsild and TimmuskВ 2005), shows a nonвҖҗsignificant decrease with age in humans. WEE2 antisense RNA 1 (WEE2вҖҗAS1, a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ593963) is a long nonвҖҗcoding RNA (lncRNA) involved in meiotic cell division (Hanna etВ al.В 2010), is reduced by CR and common proвҖҗlongevity interventions. Solute carrier family 9вҖҗmember a7 (SLC9A7, a potential target of piRвҖҗDQ597786), involved in regulation of cellular pH balance (Kagami etВ al.В 2008), decreases with aging in humans and increases with CR. These expression patterns across species and interventions underscore the biological relevance of these smRNA targets in modulating lifespan.

Using Database for Annotation, Visualization, and Integrated Discovery (DAVID), we identified significant pathways through functional enrichment analysis of mRNAs predicted by survivalвҖҗassociated smRNAs (TableВ S10); these included a longevityвҖҗregulating pathway (Bonferroni pвҖү=вҖү0.0001). The overall predicted targets were enriched for transcriptional, cellвҖҗcycle, and stress/inflammatory signaling pathwaysвҖ”including MAPK, PI3KвҖ“Akt, TP53, and TLR cascadesвҖ”indicating convergence on regulatory programs that govern proliferation, apoptosis, and cellular stress responses. Additional pathway analysis focused on the 5вҖҗyear survival predictors because this time horizon included a sufficient number of identified miRNAs and could be analyzed using available tools such as MiRTarBase 9.0 for mRNA target prediction. After filtering for вҖңstrong evidenceвҖқвҖ”defined by miRTarBase as interactions validated by direct experimental assays (e.g., reporter, Western blot, or qPCR; see Methods S1)вҖ”we identified 2233 mRNAs targeted by the 18 miRNAs, with each miRNA predicted to target 12вҖ“441 mRNAs. Notably, 477 mRNAs were targeted by multiple miRNAs (FigureВ 3A). STRING network analysis (Szklarczyk etВ al.В 2019) revealed that these shared targets were highly interactive and enriched in 79 Reactome pathways. Four of the 79 pathways were senescenceвҖҗrelated; two of these, cellular senescence and oxidative stressвҖҗinduced senescence, were among the top 10 pathways (FigureВ 3B). Six pathways were apoptosisвҖҗrelated, three stressвҖҗresponseвҖҗrelated, and four immune systemвҖҗrelated (FigureВ 3C). Additionally, many targets were implicated in regulating biosynthetic processes, mitochondrial function, metabolic processes and cell death (FigureВ S3).
